concerns.text: The plan assumes the full pipeline (STAR alignment of 12-20 samples, SUPPA2 quantification, and 1000-permutation phylogenetic regression) can complete within a 6-hour window on a free-tier GitHub Actions runner (CPU-only, ≤7GB RAM). STAR alignment alone for 100M+ read paired-end samples typically requires 15-30GB RAM and 1-2 hours per sample on 8 cores. Running 12-20 samples sequentially or even in parallel on a 7GB RAM node will likely cause OOM failures or exceed the 6-hour CI timeout, making the proposed compute model infeasible for the stated scale.

Phase 7 (Enrichment Testing) requires 1,000 permutations of phylogenetic logistic regression (`phylolm::phyloglm`) per lineage. Running this on a free-tier CI runner with limited CPU time (6h total cap) is computationally prohibitive. The permutation test alone could take hours per lineage, exceeding the total allowed runtime before the statistical model even converges.
